summary: Implement MAKE-TWO-WAY-STREAM plus the TWO-WAY-STREAM-INPUT-STREAM / -OUTPUT-STREAM accessors as C builtins, with direction-aware I/O delegation.

items.title: Add two-way stream type, builtins, accessors, typep/printer support, and tests
items.description:
Implement CL two-way streams in C, mirroring the existing synonym-stream pattern (the CL_Stream struct already has two GC-tracked CL_Obj fields, string_buf and element_type, which the synonym stream reuses for delegation).

1. include/types via src/core/types.h: add a new stream type constant `#define CL_STREAM_TWO_WAY 6` next to the existing CL_STREAM_SOCKET (line ~422). Do NOT add new struct fields — reuse string_buf for the input child and element_type for the output child.

2. src/core/stream.h: declare `CL_Obj cl_make_two_way_stream(CL_Obj input_stream, CL_Obj output_stream);` near cl_make_synonym_stream.

3. src/core/stream.c:
   - Add `cl_make_two_way_stream`: allocate via `cl_make_stream(CL_STREAM_IO, CL_STREAM_TWO_WAY)`, then set `st->string_buf = input_stream;` and `st->element_type = output_stream;`. (No GC protection needed beyond the existing pattern — both args are GC-rooted in the caller's args[] and no allocation occurs after cl_make_stream returns; follow cl_make_synonym_stream's style.)
   - Add a new direction-aware resolver alongside resolve_synonym, e.g. `static CL_Obj resolve_stream(CL_Obj stream, int writing)` that loops: if NIL or !CL_STREAM_P -> return CL_NIL; if stream_type==CL_STREAM_SYNONYM -> follow `cl_symbol_value(st->string_buf)`; if stream_type==CL_STREAM_TWO_WAY -> follow `writing ? st->element_type : st->string_buf`; otherwise return stream. This uniformly handles nested synonym/two-way.
   - Replace the `resolve_synonym(stream)` call at the top of the I/O functions with `resolve_stream(stream, <dir>)`: cl_stream_read_char (dir=0), cl_stream_read_byte (dir=0), cl_stream_peek_char (dir=0), cl_stream_unread_char (dir=0), cl_stream_write_char (dir=1), cl_stream_write_byte (dir=1), cl_stream_write_string (dir=1). The rest of each function then operates on the resolved child whose own direction/flags are checked as today.
   - Leave cl_stream_close using resolve_synonym (NOT resolve_stream): per CLHS, closing a two-way stream must NOT close its constituent streams; the TYPE_STREAM/default case in the close switch already just clears the OPEN flag, which is correct.

4. src/core/builtins_stream.c:
   - Add `bi_make_two_way_stream(args,n)`: require args[0] and args[1] be CL_STREAM_P (cl_error CL_ERR_TYPE with a clear message otherwise); validate args[0] is input-capable (direction INPUT or IO) and args[1] is output-capable (direction OUTPUT or IO) for good diagnostics; return `cl_make_two_way_stream(args[0], args[1])`.
   - Add `bi_two_way_stream_input_stream(args,n)`: require CL_STREAM_P and stream_type==CL_STREAM_TWO_WAY (else cl_error), return `st->string_buf`.
   - Add `bi_two_way_stream_output_stream(args,n)`: same checks, return `st->element_type`.
   - Register in cl_builtins_stream_init via defun: "MAKE-TWO-WAY-STREAM" (2,2), "TWO-WAY-STREAM-INPUT-STREAM" (1,1), "TWO-WAY-STREAM-OUTPUT-STREAM" (1,1). The symbols are already interned/exported in builtins.c, so defun simply attaches the function.

5. src/core/builtins_type.c (line ~224): change the `TWO-WAY-STREAM` branch so it no longer always returns 0 — make `(typep x 'two-way-stream)` return `CL_STREAM_P(obj) && ((CL_Stream *)CL_OBJ_TO_PTR(obj))->stream_type == CL_STREAM_TWO_WAY`. Keep BROADCAST-STREAM / CONCATENATED-STREAM / ECHO-STREAM returning 0 (still unimplemented). (`(typep x 'stream)` already works since CL_STREAM_P is true.)

6. src/core/printer.c (TYPE_STREAM case, line ~1300): add a `case CL_STREAM_TWO_WAY:` that prints `#<TWO-WAY-STREAM>` cleanly (special-case so it doesn't fall through to the generic type-prefix + direction suffix that would yield `#<IO-STREAM>`). Keep the closed-flag suffix.

7. tests/test_stream.c: add host tests using cl_eval_string and the cl_make_* helpers covering: make-two-way-stream returns a stream; streamp / input-stream-p / output-stream-p all T; two-way-stream-input-stream and -output-stream return the exact constituent streams; reading consumes from the input child and writing accumulates on the output child (e.g. build from make-string-input-stream + make-string-output-stream, read-char/read-line from it, write-char/write-string to it, then get-output-stream-string on the output child); (typep tw 'two-way-stream), (typep tw 'stream) true and (typep tw 'synonym-stream) false; accessors on a non-two-way stream signal an error. Register each TEST in the runner block at the bottom (near RUN(make_string_input_stream)).

8. tests/amiga/run-tests.lisp: add `check` assertions in the stream section (near line ~1717-1765) mirroring the host tests: streamp/input-stream-p/output-stream-p, accessor identity, read-from-input / write-to-output round-trip via get-output-stream-string, and the typep cases. These run unchanged on host and Amiga.

Verify with `make test` (must pass). The change is pure C builtins — no boot.lisp/FASL regeneration is required.
